'''Ryan O'Hara''' (born 18 August 1980) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played for the [[Jacksonville Axemen]] in the [[USA Rugby League]]. A New South Wales [[State of Origin]] representative forward, he previously played in the [[NRL]] for the [[Canberra Raiders]] and the [[Wests Tigers]].<ref name="encyc">{{cite book|author=Alan Whiticker & Glen Hudson|title=The Encyclopedia of Rugby League Players|page=407|year=2007 
|publisher=Gary Allen Pty Ltd|location=[[Wetherill Park, New South Wales]]|isbn=978-1-877082-93-1}}</ref> He played as a {{rlp|pr}}.

==Background==
O'Hara was born in [[Sydney, New South Wales]], Australia.

==Playing career==
A [[Hunter Mariners]] junior,<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20110518112542/http://www.rl1908.com/Clubs/Hunter-Mariners.htm Hunter Mariners] ''1908.com''</ref> O'Hara made his NRL début as a 20-year-old and won the Canberra Raiders' ''Rookie of the Year'' award in 2001. He made his representative début for Country in the City v Country Origin match of 2003. The following year he was selected to represent [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] in Game I of the [[2004 State of Origin series]].<ref name="encyc" /> In his first four seasons with the Raiders, he played in 92 games, but only made 3 appearances in 2005.

Having joined the premiership-winning Wests Tigers at the close of the [[2005 NRL season]], O'Hara first played for them in the [[2006 World Club Challenge]] loss to [[Bradford Bulls]] in England. He was bought by coach [[Tim Sheens]] to add some size to the small Wests Tigers pack and was a regular in first grade in 2006, playing in 22 games. For the next two seasons he managed just eight appearances, and was later described as one of the worst buys in NRL history.<ref>{{cite web| work=Rugby League Week| title=40 Worst Buys in NRL History| url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/40-worst-nrl-buys-30-21/|access-date=27 December 2014|date=23 November 2014}}</ref> He played four games straight in 2007, all victories, before having his jaw broken. He returned to lower grades towards the end of the season, before having his jaw broken again at the start of 2008, and didn't return to first grade until round 22.<ref>{{cite news| work= Courier Mail| title=Ryan O'Hara making up for lost time| first = Christian| last = Nicolussi| url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/ohara-making-up-for-lost-time/story-e6frepao-1111117195893|access-date=27 December 2014|date=15 August 2008}}</ref>

At the end of the [[2008 NRL season]] O'Hara moved to Wales to play in the [[Super League]] for the Crusaders. He spent three seasons at the club before joining Hull KR. O'Hara played 15 games for Hull in 2012, but suffered an [[Achilles tendon]] injury in the 2013 pre-season. Months later he was still described as being out indefinitely,<ref>{{cite news| work= Hull Daily Mail| title=Hull KR: No target date for Ryan O'Hara's return| url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Hull-KR-target-date-Ryan-O-Hara-s-return/story-18341802-detail/story.html|access-date=27 December 2014|date=7 March 2013}}</ref> and made no further appearances for the club. Announcing his departure to [[Lézignan Sangliers]], O'Hara said, "I've had a good time in England and Wales for the last five seasons. My two years here at Hull KR have been quite upsetting for me though because of the injuries I've had. That’s nobody’s fault, just bad luck really."<ref>{{cite web| work= Rovers News| title=O'Hara heading for France| url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/ohara-heading-for-france|access-date=27 December 2014|date=31 July 2013}}</ref>

Later in 2014, O'Hara joined [[The Entrance Tigers]] in the [[Ron Massey Cup]], co-captaining them to a grand final victory.<ref>{{cite news| work= Daily Telegraph| title=The Entrance Tigers scored an extra-time 22-18 win over Mounties to claim the Ron Massey Cup title in their final season in the NSW Rugby League competition on Sunday| first = Steven| last = Maher| url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/newslocal/central-coast/the-entrance-tigers-scored-an-extratime-2218-win-over-mounties-to-claim-the-ron-massey-cup-title-in-their-final-season-in-the-nsw-rugby-league-competition-on-sunday/story-fngr8h0p-1227073871663|access-date=27 December 2014|date=29 September 2014}}</ref>

===Highlights===
*''First Grade Debut:'' Round 3, Canberra v Cronulla at Toyota Park, 3 March 2001.
*''State of Origin:'' Selected for New South Wales, in game I of the [[2004 Rugby League State of Origin series|2004 State of Origin]] series.
